event: no history of drug addiction or intoxication
blood and other body fluid cultures were sterile
mild fatty infiltration of the liver
treated with tablet doxycycline 100 mg twice daily
weakness progressed to the upper limbs (Grade 2/5)
shifted to the Intensive Care Unit
magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) of the brain was normal
MRI of the cervical spine was normal
nerve conduction velocity showed motor sensory demyelinating polyneuropathy
cerebrospinal fluid analysis did not reveal albuminocytologic dissociation
started on intravenous immunoglobulin therapy 0.4 mg/kg/day for 5 days
tablet rifampicin 600 mg twice daily for meningoencephalitis
improvement in weakness of all four limbs
aggressive limb and chest physiotherapy was continued
followed up regularly in the outpatient department
